---
title: "ENVS 193DS Infographic"
date: "June 6, 2026" 
author: "Danielle Sur" 
format: pdf 
editor: source
---

# Set up

```{r}
#| message: false

# loaded in packages
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(here)
library(webr)

# read in data and saved as object called `rxntime`
rxntime <- read_csv("data/rxn-time.csv")
```

Data last updated on May 27, 2026.

# Part 3. Visualizing data

## a. Cleaning data

```{r}
rxntime_clean <- rxntime |> 
  clean_names()
```

### Pie chart

```{r}

```

### Strip plot

```{r}

```

### Timeseries plot

```{r}
# base layer: ggplot
ggplot(data = rxntime_clean,
       # x-axis: date
       # y-axis: rxntime
       # color, line type, and size by region
       mapping = aes(x = date_mm_dd_yyyy,
                     y = reaction_time_avg_ms,
                     color = exercise_type,
                     linetype = exercise_type,
                     linewidth = exercise_type)) +
  # adding lines
  geom_line() + 
  # relabeling axes
  # adding title and subtitle
  labs( x = "Date",
        y = "Mean Reaction Time (ms)",
        color = "exercise_type",
        title = "Mean Reaction Times by Exercise Type") +
  # custom colors
  scale_color_manual(values =
                       c("Biking" = "red",
                         "Volleyball" = "green4")) +
  # custom line type
  scale_linetype_manual(values =
                          c("Biking" = "dotted",
                            "Volleyball" = "solid")) +
  # custom line sizes
  scale_linewidth_manual(values = 
                      c("Biking" = 1,
                        "Volleyball" = 0.7)) +
  # adjusting figure presentation
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45), # angling x-axis text
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, # repositioning y-axis title
                                vjust = 0.6, # y-axis title placement
                                size = 8, # y-axis title size
                                margin = margin(t = 20,
                                                r = 0,
                                                b = 0,
                                                l = 0)),
    # x-axis title size
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 8),
    # plot title adjustments
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0, # shifting plot title to the left
                              size = 10), # changing plot title size
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8), # changing plot subtitle size
    legend.position = c(1, 1), # legend to the top right
    legend.justification = c(1, 1), # justifying legend
    panel.background = element_blank(), # removing panel background
    axis.line.y = element_blank(), # no y-axis line
    panel.grid = element_blank(), # removing panel grid
    axis.ticks = element_blank(), # removing axis ticks
    legend.title = element_blank() # removing the legend title
  )
```

# Part 4. Write about your process

A classmate on Friday used birds to represent raw data points.
